mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals a tightening labor market that raises wage pressure and inflation expectations, prompting the Fed to raise rates and pushing the 10-year yield higher over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and 20-day yield change remains below 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or yield falls in 60% of instances with payroll growth >150k.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 4.5%, the labor market is sufficiently tight to generate inflation pressure through wage-price spirals, causing the 10-year yield to rise over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: 20-day yield declines in more than half of instances when UNRATE is below 4.5%, or the average yield change is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of rising inflation and a rising federal funds rate amplifies the impact on long yields: higher inflation erodes bond purchasing power while rate hikes signal tighter policy, together driving 10-year yields up.
failure_condition: Cross-sectional rank correlation of the interaction term (CPIAUCSL change * FEDFUNDS level) with 20-day yield change below 0.04 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During economic expansions (GDPC1 above trend), robust industrial production growth reflects strong aggregate demand that pushes up real rates and inflation expectations, causing the 10-year yield to rise within the month.
failure_condition: Average 20-day yield change is negative or zero in at least half of instances where GDP YoY >2.5% and INDPRO growth >0.5% MoM.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in housing starts reduces demand for credit and signals a cooling economy, lowering growth expectations and causing the 10-year yield to fall over a one-week horizon as flight-to-safety sets in.
failure_condition: Rank IC of HOUST momentum with 5-day yield change is above -0.04 (i.e., not negative enough) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly retail sales growth exceeds 0.8% month-over-month, it signals strong consumer spending that boosts GDP growth expectations and inflation, leading to a rise in the 10-year yield over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day yield change is negative in more than 40% of triggered instances, or average yield change is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between low unemployment and accelerating inflation (Phillips curve mechanism) intensifies market expectations of Fed tightening, causing a larger rise in the 10-year yield than each effect alone would produce.
failure_condition: Conditional on the trigger, 20-day yield change is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p>0.10) or the interaction coefficient in a linear regression is not positive at 10% significance.

mechanism_narrative: During a period of stable Fed funds rate (no change for 3 months), a rise in inflation expectations as proxied by CPIAUCSL reduces real yields less than nominal yields, but the net effect on the 10-year yield is positive as bond markets price in future rate hikes.
failure_condition: 60-day yield change is not positively correlated with CPIAUCSL YoY change in periods when FEDFUNDS is stable (correlation <0.2) over any 5-year window.
